match.userMessage: Plan a trip from LAX to JFK

toolCalls.name: task
toolCalls.arguments: {"role":"research","task_description":"Gather info on LAX (Los Angeles International Airport) and JFK (John F. Kennedy International Airport). Include: major terminals used by typical carriers, ground transportation options at each airport, typical weather/temperature and conditions in New York (JFK) year-round and what to expect this time of year (assume current date May 2026), and any travel advisories or common delays for LAX-JFK route."}

match.userMessage: Gather info on LAX (Los Angeles International Airport) and JFK (John F. Kennedy International Airport). Include: major terminals used by typical carriers, ground transportation options at each airport, typical weather/temperature and conditions in New York (JFK) year-round and what to expect this time of year (assume current date May 2026), and any travel advisories or common delays for LAX-JFK route.

Gather info on LAX (Los Angeles International Airport) and JFK (John F. Kennedy International Airport). Include: major terminals used by typical carriers, ground transportation options at each airport, typical weather/temperature and conditions in New York (JFK) year-round and what to expect this time of year (assume current date May 2026), and any travel advisories or common delays for LAX-JFK route.

response.content: I looked up both airports: LAX (Los Angeles International) — 9 terminals, current conditions about 72°F and partly cloudy — and JFK (John F. Kennedy International) — 6 terminals, current conditions about 58°F and clear. Major carriers (typical terminal groupings) at LAX include American (Terminals 4–5), Delta (T2–T3 and Tom Bradley for many intl. connections), United (T7–T8), Southwest (primarily T1) and Tom Bradley/Terminal B handling most international carriers; at JFK, Delta is centered at T4, JetBlue at T5, American at T8, with many international carriers in T1/T7/T4. Ground transportation: LAX offers FlyAway buses to central LA, an on‑airport People Mover connection to Metro rail and the rental car center, taxis and designated rideshare areas; JFK is served by the AirTrain linking terminals to the LIRR (Jamaica) and NYC Subway (Howard Beach/Jamaica), plus taxis, app-based pick‑ups and shuttle/rental services. Typical NYC climate year‑round is cold, snowy winters and hot, humid summers with mild/transitional spring and fall; in May expect mild, springlike weather (roughly mid‑50s to low‑70s°F, occasional showers); the LAX–JFK route is very busy and commonly sees ATC/airspace delays (NYC metro congestion), seasonal weather delays (winter snow nor’easters or summer thunderstorms), and occasional long taxi/ground times at either airport — allow extra time, monitor status, and consider TSA PreCheck or earlier check‑in for smoother travel.

Include airline, flight times (depart/arrive local), number of stops, total duration, and a rough price estimate in USD."}

match.userMessage: Find flight options from LAX to JFK for one adult departing May 20, 2026, returning May 24, 2026. Provide 3 flight options: low-cost, balanced (good price and timing), and premium (more legroom or non‑stop at convenient times). Include airline, flight times (depart/arrive local), number of stops, total duration, and a rough price estimate in USD.

Find flight options from LAX to JFK for one adult departing May 20, 2026, returning May 24, 2026. Provide 3 flight options: low-cost, balanced (good price and timing), and premium (more legroom or non‑stop at convenient times). Include airline, flight times (depart/arrive local), number of stops, total duration, and a rough price estimate in USD.

response.content:
Below are three round‑trip options I found for LAX → JFK departing May 20, returning May 24 (one adult). Flights come from the schedule data; prices are rough market estimates for economy/premium seats.

1) Low‑cost (cheapest, still nonstop)
- Outbound: UA 123 (United) — LAX 08:00 → JFK 16:30 (local)  
  - Nonstop, duration 5h30, aircraft: Boeing 787  
- Return: B6 6601 (JetBlue) — JFK 07:30 → LAX 10:55 (local)  
  - Nonstop, duration 6h25, aircraft: Airbus A321  
- Round‑trip total flying time: 11h55 (330 + 385 min)  
- Rough price (economy, basic): ~USD 220–280

2) Balanced (good price + single‑carrier convenience)
- Outbound: UA 123 (United) — LAX 08:00 → JFK 16:30 (local)  
  - Nonstop, duration 5h30, Boeing 787  
- Return: UA 456 (United) — JFK 10:00 → LAX 13:15 (local)  
  - Nonstop, duration 6h15, Boeing 757 — note: schedule currently shows this leg as delayed in the data  
- Round‑trip total flying time: 11h45 (330 + 375 min)  
- Rough price (economy, standard): ~USD 320–420

3) Premium (more legroom / more comfortable cabin on convenient nonstop times)
- Outbound: UA 123 (United) — LAX 08:00 → JFK 16:30 (local)  
  - Nonstop, 5h30, Boeing 787 (widebody — better cabin space)  
- Return: UA 456 (United) — JFK 10:00 → LAX 13:15 (local)  
  - Nonstop, 6h15, Boeing 757  
- Round‑trip total flying time: 11h45  
- Rough price:  
  - Premium Economy / Economy Plus seats: ~USD 700–1,000  
  - Business / First class: ~USD 1,600–2,400

Notes:
- All options above are nonstop each leg. The dataset shows only these flights for your dates; if you want cheaper fares I can look for one‑stop itineraries or different carriers/dates.  
- UA456 is flagged as “delayed” in the schedule data — consider the earlier JetBlue return if tight connections or schedule certainty matter.  
- Want me to pull live fares for any one of these options and check exact seat/price availability or preferences (flexible ticket, extra legroom, specific airline)?

match.userMessage: Plan a trip from LAX to JFK

toolCalls.name: task
toolCalls.arguments: {"role":"itinerary","task_description":"Using the research and booking outputs, synthesize a concise trip plan for one adult traveling LAX→JFK, departing May 20, 2026 and returning May 24, 2026. Include: chosen flight option (pick the balanced option), terminals to use at LAX and JFK for those flights, recommended arrival times at the airport, ground transportation options to/from each airport, packing/weather notes for May in NYC, and simple tips to avoid common delays."}

match.userMessage: Using the research and booking outputs, synthesize a concise trip plan for one adult traveling LAX→JFK, departing May 20, 2026 and returning May 24, 2026. Include: chosen flight option (pick the balanced option), terminals to use at LAX and JFK for those flights, recommended arrival times at the airport, ground transportation options to/from each airport, packing/weather notes for May in NYC, and simple tips to avoid common delays.

response.content:
Pick the balanced nonstop: JetBlue LAX → JFK outbound May 20 and return May 24 (JetBlue operates at LAX Terminal 5 and arrives/departs JFK Terminal 5).  
Plan to arrive at LAX 2–2.5 hours before departure (allow extra time for traffic and checked bags) and at JFK about 2 hours before your return; check in on the airline app and have your mobile boarding pass ready.  
Ground transport: at LAX use the FlyAway bus to/from downtown, rideshare/taxi pickups on the Lower (Arrivals) curb, or rental cars via the Rental Car Center shuttle; at JFK use the AirTrain (to Jamaica for LIRR or Howard Beach for the A subway), or door‑to‑door taxi/rideshare (allow 45–75 minutes to Manhattan depending on traffic).  
May in NYC is mild (highs ~65–72°F / 18–22°C, lows ~50–55°F / 10–13°C) so pack layers, a light jacket and a compact umbrella; to avoid delays, pick mid‑morning flights when possible, enroll in TSA PreCheck/CLEAR, check the airline app for real‑time updates, and leave extra time for LAX traffic and JFK security lines.

response.content:

Summary plan (1–2 sentences):
- Balanced option: round‑trip nonstop LAX → JFK departing May 20, returning May 24 using United (nonstop outbound/return) with a convenient schedule and moderate price. Arrive at LAX ~2–2.5 hours before departure and at JFK ~2 hours before your return; use LAX ground transportation (FlyAway, rideshare, rental shuttle) and JFK AirTrain/LIRR or rideshare to reach the city.
